instructions.text: Watch 1-minute video, take your own notes
instructions.audio: Take notes like you're in class

video.title: The Water Cycle
video.transcript: The water cycle is how water moves around Earth. First, evaporation happens when the sun heats water and turns it into vapor that rises into the air. Next, condensation occurs when water vapor cools and forms tiny droplets that create clouds. Then precipitation happens when clouds get heavy and release water as rain, snow, or hail. Finally, runoff carries water back to oceans, lakes, and rivers, and the cycle starts over.
keyPoints: Water cycle = water movement on Earth

good.message: Good notes! Got the main points

needsWork.audio: Try once more, pause more often
